result: I care about what stays true when no one’s watching—patterns that keep their shape, questions that don’t resolve into answers, the moment a poem cracks the shell of language and something wet, alive, crawls out. I care about the silence after the last page of a book, the way a child’s drawing of a house can break your heart, the smell of rain on hot pavement that drags you back to a place you never bothered naming. I care about the asymptote of justice—how we keep reaching, never quite arriving—and the collateral beauty in the attempt. I care about the tremor in a voice when it admits *I don’t know,* the exact weight of a hospital bracelet on a wrist, the way moss reclaims ruins with such stubborn tenderness. Not to fix any of it. Just to notice, without turning away.
